events.title: Basquiat × Banksy - Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
events.description: Basquiat×Banksyis an exhibition of two major paintings, one by Jean-Michel Basquiat (b. Brooklyn, New York, 1960–1988) and the other by Banksy (anonymous; b. near Bristol, England). Placed in dialogue, Basquiat’sBoy and Dog in a Johnnypump(1982) and Banksy’s response,Banksquiat. Boy and Dog in Stop and Search(2018), reveal throughlines among street art, contemporary art, and the popular imagination.
events.start_location: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
events.organizer: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
events.artists: Art & Artists, Art & Artists, Search the Collection, Art & Artists, Jean, Banksy, either artist has been presented at the nation, Basquiat between, Richard, Betsy Johnson

events.title: Revolutions: Art from the Hirshhorn Collection, 1860–1960 - Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
events.description: In celebration of its 50th-anniversary season, the Smithsonian’s Hirshhorn Museum presentsRevolutions: Art from the Hirshhorn Collection, 1860–1960, a major survey of artwork made during a transformative period characterized by new currents in science and philosophy and ever-increasing mechanization.Revolutionscaptures shifting cultural landscapes through the largely chronological presentation of 208 artworks made by 122 artists—including Francis Bacon, Jean Dubuffet, Lee Krasner, Wilfredo Lam, Georgia O’Keeffe, Pablo Picasso, and Jackson Pollock—during these turbulent and energetic years. Filling the Outer Ring galleries on the Museum’s second floor, the works are all part of the Hirshhorn’s permanent collection, and many were gifts to the nation from founding donor Joseph H. Hirshhorn.
events.start_location: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
events.organizer: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
events.artists: Art & Artists, Art & Artists, Search the Collection, Show more, new currents in science and philosophy and ever, Hirshhorn Curator Marina Isgro and Assistant Curator Betsy Johnson, the Hirshhorn International Council and Hirshhorn Collectors, Paris, free public programs, women artists on view in Revolutions

events.title: Mark Bradford: Pickett’s Charge - Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
events.description: “Politically and socially, we are at the edge of another precipice. I’m standing in the middle of a question about where we are as a nation.”—Mark Bradford
events.start_location: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
events.organizer: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
